LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PREMISED ON THE ALLEGATION THAT DEFENDANT HAD A CONTRACTUAL OBLIGATION TO GRANT PLAINTIFFS LOAN MODIFICATION IF PLAINTIFFS MADE PAYMENTS UNDER THE FOREBEARANCE AGREEMENT. THE CLEAR LANGUAGE OF THE AGREEMENT PROVIDES THAT THERE WAS NOTHING AUTOMATIC IN THE GRANTING OF A MODIFICATION. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE A BREACH BY THE REFUSAL TO GRANT WITHOUT SPECIFYING ANY SUCH LANGUAGE IN THE AGREEMENT. THE COVENANT OF GOOD FAITH AND FAIR DEALING CANNOT IMPOSE SUBSTANTIVE DUTIES ON THE CONTRACTING PARTIES BEYOND THOSE INCORPORATED IN THE SPECIFIC TERMS OF THE AGREEMENT. THE UCL CLAIM CANNOT BE MAINTAINED IN THE ABSENCE OF ANY OTHER LAW SUCCESSFULLY ALLEGED TO HAVE BEEN VIOLATED BY DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings , Points And Authoritiesand Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Sep-15-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT AURORA LOAN SERVICES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted with leave to amend. All four causes of action are premised on the allegation that defendant had a contractual obligation to grant plaintiffs loan modification if plaintiffs made payments under the forebearance agreement. The clear language of the agreement provides that there was nothing automatic in the granting of a modification. Plaintiffs allege a breach by the refusal to grant without specifying any such language in the agreement. The covenant of good faith and fair dealing cannot impose substantive duties on the contracting parties beyond those incorporated in the specific terms of the agreement. The UCL claim cannot be maintained in the absence of any other law successfully alleged to have been violated by defendant. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
